name: feature-probe
$comment: Phase 6 wire shape: _action carries {name} only. Parameters that used to ride in `context` (redirect URL, storage values, download URL/filename, form note, sort column/direction, table filter, page number) now live in dedicated state slots on FeatureProbeState (Plan 06-04). The fixture writes them via stateMutations to mimic the renderer's bind writes that would happen before each user click. Table sort uses per-column action names (table-sort-name / table-sort-status); pagination uses prevAction/nextAction (table-page-prev / table-page-next). 1.11.0: buildVm now also renders a layout:"row" section (with LinkNode children); it is static view-shape captured by every GET step (initial/fresh-*), so the byte-identical diff across .NET/bun/node covers the new SectionNode field without a dedicated action step. 1.12.0: buildVm additionally renders the arrange/align alignment vocabulary as static view-shape — a bare layout:"row" section with NEITHER arrange nor align (proving omitted = absent on the wire), the canonical header-bar (row + arrange:"space-between" + a heading TextNode first child + a nested row nav cluster, ALIGN-04), one row per remaining arrange value (start/center/end/space-around/space-evenly), and one row per align value (start/center/end/stretch/baseline). All captured by the existing GET steps, so the byte-identical diff covers PageNode/SectionNode.arrange + .align with no new step. 1.13.0: buildVm additionally renders the switcher vocabulary as static view-shape — a bare layout:"switcher" section with NEITHER threshold nor limit (proving omitted = absent on the wire), one switcher per threshold value (sm/md/lg/xl), and a switcher with limit:4 set (6 LinkNode children). All captured by the existing GET steps, so the byte-identical diff covers PageNode/SectionNode.layout:"switcher" + .threshold + .limit with no new step. 1.13.0 also: buildVm renders the cards minItem vocabulary as static view-shape — a SECTION-level bare layout:"cards" section with NO minItem (proving omitted = absent on the wire, even though the page root is already layout:"cards"), and one layout:"cards" section per minItem value (xs/sm/md/lg/xl). All captured by the existing GET steps, so the byte-identical diff covers PageNode/SectionNode.minItem with no new step. Phase 10 (fits node): buildVm additionally renders the new fits node as static view-shape — a fits with `axis` OMITTED (proving omitted = absent on the wire) and a fits with `axis:"both"` (proving the axis field present as the JSON string "both"), each wrapped in a labeled section and carrying preferred-first/fallback-last candidate children (a layout:"row" candidate first, a layout:"stack" candidate last). All captured by the existing GET steps, so the byte-identical diff covers the {type:"fits", axis?, children} wire with no new step. NOTE: the fits node's CLIENT-SIDE measure-and-pick selection is browser-only (ResizeObserver + DOM layout in BrowserAdapter) and is NOT part of parity — parity proves only that the node serializes identically across backends. 2.1.0 (LinkNode.active): the canonical header-bar nav cluster marks its first link active:true ("you are here") in both backends; the remaining links omit active (proving omitted = absent on the wire, matching the .NET bool?+WhenWritingNull posture). Captured by the existing GET steps, so the byte-identical diff covers LinkNode.active with no new step. 3.0.0 (unified appearance axes): buildVm renders an "Appearance axes" section as static view-shape covering the new vocabulary — button emphasis/tone/size (+ a primary×danger×lg combo and a copy-button with all three), section tone (a card+tone and a bare tone), text tone (+ heading×tone), list-item state+tone, and table-row state+tone. The old fused `variant` is gone (buttons→emphasis/tone, list/row→state/tone, text style→tone). Captured by the existing GET steps, so the byte-identical diff covers every new field with no new step. 3.2.0 (child-side modifiers): buildVm renders a "Child modifiers (alignSelf + maxWidth)" section as static view-shape — a bare variant:"card" child (NEITHER alignSelf nor maxWidth => proves omitted = absent on the wire), one card per alignSelf value (start/center/end), one card per maxWidth value (half/two-thirds/three-quarters/prose), and a two-bubble chat composition (alignSelf + maxWidth + tone). Captured by the existing GET steps, so the byte-identical diff covers SectionNode.alignSelf + .maxWidth with no new step. Feedback primitives: buildVm renders a 'Feedback primitives' section as static view-shape — three BadgeNodes (bare => tone/emphasis absent, tone-only, tone+emphasis) and two EmptyStateNodes (bare => message/action absent, and one with message + a CTA ButtonNode whose unique action name feedback-cta is reached by the action-name walk descending into empty-state.action). Captured by the existing GET steps. The new trigger-toast POST step returns TWO toast side-effects (a bare message-only toast and a full tone+durationMs toast), byte-diffing the {type:'toast', message, tone?, durationMs?} side-effect wire across all backends (the toast's client-side display is browser-only and NOT part of parity). 4.0.0 (file uploadOn routing): the File-field section renders a second file field 'upload-routed' with uploadOn:["probe-submit"] alongside the bare 'upload-nobind' (proving uploadOn omitted = absent on the wire vs. present as a JSON string array). Captured by the existing GET steps, so the byte-identical diff covers FieldNode.uploadOn with no new step (the browser's uploadOn-based file collection is browser-only and NOT part of parity). Phase 12 / reshaped Phase 18 (CHARTBASE-04, chart node): buildVm additionally renders a `chart (bar)` section with two ChartNodes over the reshaped multi-series wire {type:"chart", kind?, labels, series:[{name,data,tone?}], stacked?, title?}. The first ChartNode carries `kind` OMITTED (proving omitted = absent on the wire, default "bar"), shared `labels` (Mon/Tue/Wed), two series over WHOLE-NUMBER data so double/number serialize byte-identically (12 not 12.0) — "Visits" (12/19/7, no tone, framework-assigned palette slot) and "Errors" (1/3/2, tone:"danger", a semantic override) — `stacked:true`, and a title ("Weekly visits"). The second ChartNode sets `kind:"line"` explicitly (proving the literal string crosses the wire) with a single series ("Trend", 5/10/15) and stacked/title omitted. Captured by the existing GET steps, so the byte-identical diff covers the reshaped labels/series/tone/stacked wire with no new step. NOTE: the ChartNode's CLIENT-SIDE Chart.js pixels (bars/colors/title/legend) are browser-only and NOT part of parity — parity proves only that the node serializes identically across backends. Phase 14 (NBA-04): buildVm additionally renders a 'Non-blocking actions (blocking field)' section as static view-shape — a button whose action OMITS blocking (proves omitted = absent on the wire) and a button whose action sets blocking:false (proves the literal JSON boolean false). All captured by the existing GET steps, so the byte-identical diff covers ActionEvent.blocking/ActionDescriptor.Blocking with no new step. NOTE: the CLIENT-SIDE coalescing/epoch behavior this field enables is NOT parity-tested (pure client-only mechanics per the design of record — no wire epoch, no server-side reconciliation state); parity proves only that the blocking field itself serializes identically across backends. Jump-to-page (MQ4-01): buildTableSection additionally sets Pagination.JumpAction ("table-page-jump"), exercised by two new POST steps after tbl-filter-clear — tbl-page-jump-valid (a valid direct jump to the last page, 3) and tbl-page-jump-clamp (an agent supplying an out-of-range page, 99, directly to the tablePage bind) — proving the pre-existing server-side clamp (Math.Clamp/Math.min+Math.max in Window()/tableWindow()) renders the same clamped page identically across dotnet-probe, bun-probe, and node-probe. v5.0 (ButtonNode.confirm): the Appearance-axes button list renders a 'confirm-guard' ButtonNode carrying tone:"danger" + confirm:"Delete this? This cannot be undone." (a destructive-action guard). Captured by the existing GET steps, so the byte-identical diff covers ButtonNode.confirm (WhenWritingNull => omitted absent on the wire). NOTE: the CLIENT-SIDE native window.confirm() gate this field triggers is browser-only and NOT part of parity — parity proves only that the confirm string serializes identically across backends. v5.1 (NAV-01/NAV-02, BreadcrumbNode + StepsNode): buildVm additionally renders a 'Navigation primitives' section as static view-shape — a breadcrumb whose crumbs cover the full omitted-vs-present matrix (an href-only crumb with external OMITTED => absent, an external:true crumb present as the literal boolean, an action crumb whose UNIQUE name nav-crumb-probe proves the action-name uniqueness walk DESCENDS into breadcrumb items, and a final label-only crumb the framework auto-renders as the current page), plus two StepsNodes — the first OMITS orientation (proves absent = default horizontal) and mixes a description-bearing step with two bare ones (description omitted => absent), the second sets orientation:"vertical" (proves the literal string crosses) — both with a mid current:1 (0 is meaningful, so current always crosses). All captured by the existing GET steps, so the byte-identical diff covers the {type:"breadcrumb", items:[{label, href?, external?, action?}]} and {type:"steps", steps:[{label, description?}], current, orientation?} wire with no new POST step. NOTE: the CLIENT-SIDE appearance/a11y (separators, numbered markers, connector lines, intrinsic horizontal→vertical reflow, aria-current) is browser-only and NOT part of parity — parity proves only that the two nodes serialize identically across backends.
